message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Latin America was one of the last regions of the world to become affected by the COVID-19 pandemic. As a response to the emergency virtual education was implemented in almost every country of the region.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>a massive open online course about epilepsy was made using only free software and platforms following the international league against epilepsy competence-based domains for epileptology teaching.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>250 healthcare students signed up for the course and only 17.2% of them had previously participated in courses like this one. This course had a completion rate of 37.2% and of the students that completed the course 98.3% would participate in course like this in the future.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>In conclusion MOOCs can be easily implemented as a powerful pedagogic strategy during the COVID-19 pandemic and can have a positive impact not only in in its proposed learning objectives but it can help closing the gap that prevent Latin American healthcare students to acquire actively knowledge trough them.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Personal, Professional, and Institutional Social Network accounts</jats:title>
                  <jats:list list-type="bullet">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>
                        <jats:bold>Facebook:</jats:bold>
                        <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="https://www.facebook.com/davidfabianrm">https://www.facebook.com/davidfabianrm</jats:ext-link>
                      </jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>
                        <jats:bold>Facebook:</jats:bold>
                        <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="https://www.facebook.com/Grupo-de-Investigaci%C3%B3n-en-Epidemiolog%C3%ADa-Cl%C3%ADnica-de-Colombia-GRECO-107921290887449">https://www.facebook.com/Grupo-de-Investigaci%C3%B3n-en-Epidemiolog%C3%ADa-Cl%C3%ADnica-de-Colombia-GRECO-107921290887449</jats:ext-link>
                      </jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Discussion Points</jats:title>
                  <jats:list list-type="order">
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>How can a student made MOOC impact Latin American medical students?</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>Can a student made MOOC encourage medical students to use and discover new educational online offers?</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>COVID-19 pandemic can be a good time to improve the academy using new technologies and pedagogic strategies.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>MOOCs can become a powerful tool for learning and teaching worldwide during the COVID-19 pandemic.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                    <jats:list-item>
                      <jats:p>MOOCs are an attractive tool for teaching and learning during the social distancing caused by COVID-19.</jats:p>
                    </jats:list-item>
                  </jats:list>
                </jats:sec>
title: A student made MOOC for medical students during the SARS-CoV-2 pandemic
